content:
6 min read
Just now

--

Anthropic gave 60 days and a hard cutoff. Here’s the usage audit, the eval test, and the one request parameter that now returns a 400.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Towfiqu barbhuiya on UnsplashOn August 5, every request your systems still send to Claude Opus 4.1 comes back as an error. Anthropic deprecated the model on June 5 and set a hard 60-day clock — and it’s only the latest: more than ten Claude models have been retired from Anthropic’s own API in the past year.
If you run anything on Claude in production, that sentence is a line item, not a footnote. A model you pinned six months ago because it passed your tests is about to stop answering. The fix is not hard. It is just work, and it has a deadline.
What actually changed
Anthropic moved claude-opus-4-1-20250805 into its “deprecated” state on June 5 and assigned a retirement date of August 5, with claude-opus-4-8 as the recommended replacement. Deprecated means still working but no longer recommended. Retired means requests fail. There is no grace request, no soft fallback — after the date, the call returns an error.
One catch that trips up teams on cloud marketplaces: those dates apply to Anthropic-operated platforms — the Claude API, Claude Platform on AWS, and Microsoft Foundry. Amazon Bedrock and Google Cloud Vertex AI set their own…
